behaviors.description: A static prompt-template lint check rejects phase-specific instruction leakage copied from SKILL.md.
behaviors.justification: PASS1: Unit, because this is a static lint rule over template text (single parsing/validation boundary, no runtime harness/LLM needed). Counter-argument (Integration) rejected by behavior text: "static prompt-template lint check" and "copied from SKILL.md" describe lexical leakage detection, not module handoff failure.
behaviors.test_description: Test the lint function/rule with template fixtures: one containing phase-specific SKILL.md instruction text and one clean template. Assert reject/accept deterministically.

behaviors.description: The fully assembled prompt produced by the real test harness (after interpolation and fixtures) still contains no phase-instruction leakage.
behaviors.justification: PASS1: Integration, because failure appears at module boundaries (template + interpolation + fixture assembly). Counter-argument down to Unit rejected by text "fully assembled prompt" and "real test harness". Counter-argument up to System rejected: no real network/subprocess/OS-only behavior is required.
behaviors.test_description: Run prompt assembly through the real harness pipeline with representative fixtures, then assert the final assembled prompt does not include phase-instruction leakage patterns.

behaviors.description: The test invocation loads skill context from plugin/skill files and validates behavior from that context rather than from hard-coded prompt rules.
behaviors.justification: PASS1: Integration, because this checks wiring between invocation logic, skill-file loader, and prompt/test configuration. Counter-argument up to Agentic rejected by behavior text: it focuses on source of context ("loads skill context from plugin/skill files") vs hard-coded rules, not model-output quality. Counter-argument down to Unit rejected because cross-module handoff is essential.
behaviors.test_description: Invoke the test path with real plugin/skill file loading enabled; assert effective context comes from file content and that no hard-coded phase rules in test prompt are needed for the invocation to configure behavior.

behaviors.description: The harness can run the same user-style prompt against an intentionally degraded or missing SKILL.md variant without changing test prompt text.
behaviors.justification: PASS1: Integration, because this is harness configurability across context variants and prompt immutability. Counter-argument to System rejected: behavior does not require real OS/network edge semantics, only local module/file wiring. Counter-argument to Agentic rejected: capability can be validated without judging model output.
behaviors.test_description: Run two harness invocations (normal vs degraded/missing SKILL.md variant) with identical user-style prompt text; assert only context source changes and invocation succeeds in both setups.

behaviors.description: When the relevant SKILL.md section is removed or degraded, the behavioral test fails under the same user-style prompt.
behaviors.justification: PASS1: Agentic, because correctness depends on real model behavior changing when skill context regresses (deterministic stubs can hide this). Counter-argument down to Integration rejected by behavior text "behavioral test fails" under same prompt after SKILL degradation; this is exactly LLM-decision sensitivity. Counter-argument up to Workflow rejected: single behavioral evaluation can demonstrate failure without multi-agentic sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Run live-model behavioral test twice with identical user prompt: baseline SKILL.md and degraded/removed section variant. Assert the degraded variant fails structural behavior criteria while baseline passes.

behaviors.description: Raw model output is captured to a diagnostic artifact before any JSON parsing or extraction logic runs.
behaviors.justification: PASS1: Integration, because this is an ordering contract across runner, artifact writer, and parser modules. Counter-argument to Unit rejected: failure is at handoff/order between components, not one isolated function. Counter-argument to Agentic rejected: real model quality is irrelevant; a controlled response can validate pre-parse capture.
behaviors.test_description: Execute invocation pipeline with a controlled model response payload; assert raw output artifact is written first and remains available even when downstream JSON parsing intentionally fails.

behaviors.description: Assertions enforce structural behavior constraints (for example schema paths and role-specific field placement), not only keyword presence checks.
behaviors.justification: PASS1: Unit, because this is assertion logic semantics (structure-aware validator vs keyword matcher) and can be proven with fixed fixtures. Counter-argument up to Integration rejected by behavior text, which specifies assertion type, not harness interaction. If desired, Integration can complement, but minimum boundary is local validator logic.
behaviors.test_description: Test assertion helpers with crafted outputs where keywords are present but schema path/role placement is wrong; assert structural checks fail and correctly-structured payloads pass.

behaviors.description: Under live model execution, structural assertions catch regressions that a keyword-only check would miss.
behaviors.justification: PASS1: Agentic, explicitly required by "under live model execution" and by dependence on real output variation/regression patterns. Counter-argument down to Integration rejected: stubbed outputs cannot prove regressions that arise from real model decisions. Counter-argument up to Workflow rejected: behavior can be shown in a single model-evaluation step.
behaviors.test_description: Run live-model tests on prompt/cases known to produce subtle formatting/placement drift. Compare outcomes of keyword-only check vs structural assertions; assert structural assertions fail on regressions that keyword checks pass.
